ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 (OceanLotus) Cobalt Kitty chain — Office macro spawning regsvr32/mshta with remote scriptlet (squiblydoo)
ucs.description: Hunts APT32's signature post-delivery execution chain documented in the Cobra Carbon System / Cobalt Kitty campaigns: a maldoc opened in Word/Excel/Outlook spawns regsvr32.exe with /i:http (Squiblydoo), mshta.exe with an http URL, or an empty-cmdline rundll32 to stage a Cobalt Strike / Denis backdoor. This combination of office-parent + LOLBin-child + remote scriptlet retrieval is one of OceanLotus's most repeated TTPs.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hash values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe") (Processes.process_name="regsvr32.exe" Processes.process IN ("*scrobj*","*/i:http*","*/i:\\\\*","*/u*/s*/i*")) OR (Processes.process_name="mshta.exe" Processes.process IN ("*http*","*javascript:*","*vbscript:*")) OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" (Processes.process="*rundll32.exe" OR Processes.process IN ("*javascript:*","*\\AppData\\*","*\\Temp\\*"))) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host | where user!="SYSTEM" AND NOT match(user,"\\$$") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT32 / OceanLotus — Cobalt Kitty execution chain: maldoc → LOLBin scriptlet exec
let lookback = 14d;
let office_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe","wordview.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (office_parents)
| where (FileName =~ "regsvr32.exe"
         and (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/i:http","/i:\\\\","scrobj.dll")
              or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/u\s+/s\s+/i:"))
     or (FileName =~ "mshta.exe"
         and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","javascript:","vbscript:"))
     or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
         and (isempty(ProcessCommandLine)
              or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)rundll32(\.exe)?\s*$"
              or ProcessCommandLine has "javascript:"
              or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\AppData\\(Local|Roaming)\\"
              or ProcessCommandLine has @"\Temp\"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT32 / OceanLotus — Cobalt Kitty execution chain (Sentinel ASIM port)
let lookback = 14d;
let office_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe","wordview.exe"]);
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| extend parent = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         child  = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where parent in (office_parents)
| where (child == "regsvr32.exe"
         and (TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("/i:http","/i:\\\\","scrobj.dll")
              or TargetProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/u\s+/s\s+/i:"))
     or (child == "mshta.exe"
         and TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","javascript:","vbscript:"))
     or (child == "rundll32.exe"
         and (isempty(TargetProcessCommandLine)
              or TargetProcessCommandLine has "javascript:"
              or TargetProcessCommandLine has @"\AppData\"
              or TargetProcessCommandLine has @"\Temp\"))
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine,
          TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, SHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 OceanLotus Office-Parent LOLBin Scriptlet Execution
id: 7c4e2b22-3ac1-4d0a-8a3a-ap32cobaltkitty01
status: experimental
description: Detects APT32 / OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty execution chain where Office spawns regsvr32 (Squiblydoo), mshta with HTTP, or empty-cmdline rundll32.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-cobalt-kitty-apt
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.g0050
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.t1204.002
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_office:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  squiblydoo:
    Image|endswith: '\regsvr32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/i:http'
      - 'scrobj.dll'
      - '/i:\\\\'
  mshta_http:
    Image|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - 'javascript:'
      - 'vbscript:'
  rundll_susp:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - 'javascript:'
  condition: parent_office and (squiblydoo or mshta_http or rundll_susp)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate add-in installers initiated from Office (rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty operational reporting (Cybereason, ESET, FireEye) repeatedly documents exactly this chain: spear-phish maldoc → VBA → regsvr32 with remote .sct (Squiblydoo) OR mshta over HTTP OR rundll32 launching from %AppData%/%Temp% to stage Denis/Cobalt Strike. The combination of Office parentage with LOLBin children fetching remote scriptlets is far more APT32-specific than any sing

_field_issues.message: `AppData` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Local` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Roaming` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Temp` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `Timestamp`?

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `AppData` is not a canonical column of ImProcessCreate.
`Temp` is not a canonical column of ImProcessCreate.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 (OceanLotus) DLL side-loading via legitimate signed binary in user-writable directory
ucs.description: Hunts APT32's hallmark side-loading tradecraft (RasTls.exe / Symantec, mcoemcpy.exe / McAfee, wsc_proxy.exe / AVG, GoogleUpdate.exe, Word's wwlib.dll variants) where a properly signed third-party binary is dropped into AppData/ProgramData/Temp and immediately loads an unsigned DLL from the same folder — the loader pattern used to deploy KerrDown, Salgorea and OceanLotus's PhantomDLL.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.process_name) as procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Public\\*") (Filesystem.file_name IN ("RasTls.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","goopdate.dll","wwlib.dll","SymNeti.dll","vsodscpl.dll","AcroRd32.exe","AdobeUpdate.exe")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename dest as host | join type=inner host file_path [ | tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as procHash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\Temp\\*" by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_path Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.dest as host Processes.parent_process_path as file_path Processes.parent_process_name as exec_name ] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT32 / OceanLotus — signed third-party binary loading sibling unsigned DLL from user-writable path
let lookback = 14d;
let user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\"]);
let apt32_loaders = dynamic([
    "rastls.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","googleupdate.exe",
    "acrord32.exe","adobeupdate.exe","symneti.dll","goopdate.dll",
    "wwlib.dll","vsodscpl.dll","avgrsstx.dll"]);
let sus_loads =
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (user_writable)
    | where FolderPath has_any (user_writable)
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath =~ FolderPath          // DLL alongside the EXE
    | where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
    | where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (apt32_loaders)
         or tolower(FileName) in (apt32_loaders)
         or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any ("Symantec","McAfee","AVG","Google","Adobe","Microsoft")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              LoaderImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              LoaderSigner = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              LoaderSHA = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              SideloadedDLL = FolderPath,
              DllSHA = SHA256;
sus_loads
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, FolderPath, FileName,
              DropperParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropperCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
  ) on DeviceId
| where SideloadedDLL has FolderPath
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, LoaderImage, LoaderSigner, LoaderSHA,
          SideloadedDLL, DllSHA, DropperParent, DropperCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT32 / OceanLotus — signed-binary side-loading in user-writable path (Sentinel)
// Falls back to SecurityEvent 4688 + EventID 7 (Sysmon ImageLoad) where available.
let lookback = 14d;
let apt32_loaders = dynamic([
    "rastls.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","googleupdate.exe",
    "acrord32.exe","adobeupdate.exe","symneti.dll","goopdate.dll",
    "wwlib.dll","vsodscpl.dll","avgrsstx.dll"]);
let user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7   // ImageLoad
| extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Signature = tostring(EventData.Signature),
         ImageHash = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         LoadedHash = tostring(EventData.HashesLoaded)
| where Image has_any (user_writable) and ImageLoaded has_any (user_writable)
| extend ImageDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\"),
         LoadedDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| where ImageDir =~ LoadedDir                                     // sibling on disk
| where tolower(tostring(split(Image,"\\")[-1])) in (apt32_loaders)
     or tolower(tostring(split(ImageLoaded,"\\")[-1])) in (apt32_loaders)
     or Signature has_any ("Symantec","McAfee","AVG","Google","Adobe","Microsoft Corporation")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, Signature, Signed, ImageLoaded, ImageHash, LoadedHash
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading is THE OceanLotus signature — researchers (ESET, Volexity, Mandiant, BlackBerry/Cylance) attribute APT32 partly by this very loader pattern. The actor consistently abuses signed Symantec (RasTls), McAfee (mcoemcpy), AVG (wsc_proxy), Google Updater and Microsoft Word components, dropped to AppData/ProgramData/Temp alongside a malicious twin DLL — used to load KerrDown, Salgorea, De
